To maximize calories with $5, Bob needs the Meat with the **lowest cost per 100 calories** (lowest Y-axis value), so he can buy the most calories per dollar.

Looking at all red dots (Meats) from highest to lowest cost per 100 calories:
- Ribeye steak: ~$1.25/100 cal
- Chicken breast: ~$0.85/100 cal
- Lamb shoulder: ~$0.82/100 cal
- Pork chop: ~$0.78/100 cal
- Turkey: ~$0.65/100 cal
- 80% ground lamb: ~$0.56/100 cal
- Chicken wing: ~$0.43/100 cal
- 80% ground beef: ~$0.42/100 cal
- Pork belly: ~$0.38/100 cal (lowest — dot sits visibly below the $0.40 gridline)

</think>

<answer>
Pork belly. It has the lowest cost per 100 calories among all meats (~$0.38/100 cal, below the $0.40 gridline), so $5 buys the maximum number of calories from the Meats category.
</answer>
